title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
description: Without knowing the system's architecture or the types of operations performed, it’s difficult to assess the risk of a denial-of-service (DoS) attack.  If the application is not designed to handle a large number of concurrent requests or if it contains vulnerabilities that can be exploited to consume excessive resources, it could be susceptible to a DoS attack, leading to service unavailability.
attack_vector: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in the application code that cause it to consume excessive resources (e.g., CPU, memory, network bandwidth), or a large-scale attack targeting the application's infrastructure.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Employ input validation and sanitization to prevent malicious code from consuming excessive resources.
Utilize a Content Delivery Network (CDN) to distribute traffic and mitigate the impact of DoS attacks.
